brief.title: aion-awl parser split: parser.rs (1585 lines) into a parser/ folder module, allow-block PURGED by doing the deferred work (docs written, unwrap/expect eliminated, parse_step decomposed), zero behavior change, mechanically fenced scope
brief.objective: Split crates/aion-awl/src/parser.rs (1585 lines) into a folder module crates/aion-awl/src/parser/ whose mod.rs contains ONLY module declarations and re-exports, with every child file at or under 500 lines, AND purge the line-1 allow block `#![allow(missing_docs, clippy::expect_used, clippy::too_many_lines, clippy::unnecessary_wraps, clippy::unwrap_used)]` by DOING the deferred work behind it — all at ZERO behavior cost. The crate root already declares `mod parser;` (lib.rs line 10), which resolves to parser/mod.rs automatically — lib.rs therefore needs NO edit and MUST NOT be edited. The split itself is a pure move; the only logic edits permitted are those strictly required to eliminate the five bypassed lints. Structure guidance from the real seams: ParseError + its impls (public error type); the source-line model (SourceLine, SourceLines, count_indent, split_code_comment, trim_comment, span, lex_at, rebase_span); the document walk (DeclPhase, LineParser + cursor methods, parse_document/parse_workflow/parse_maybe_about/parse_io/parse_type_decl/parse_action_decl); the step machinery (parse_step, parse_handler, and the field helpers parse_each/parse_repeat/parse_string_field/parse_duration_field/parse_duration_text/parse_retry/set_op/push_leading/push_trailing/reject_duplicate); call parsing (parse_do, parse_call_text, comma_parts); the type sub-parser (TypeParser + parse_type_at); the expression sub-parser (ExprParser + parse_expr_at, plus unit_text/duration_text). Multiple impl blocks for one type across child modules of the same crate are legal Rust and the intended technique (001a split the large Ctx impl exactly this way); free helpers shared across children take `pub(super)` visibility (an internal widening, not a public-API or signature change). Choose child-module names from the actual responsibilities you find (e.g. parser/error.rs, parser/source.rs, parser/document.rs, parser/steps.rs, parser/calls.rs, parser/types.rs, parser/expressions.rs) — mechanically named files (part_00.rs, chunk_1.rs, body.rs holding everything) and include!() textual splicing are REJECTED outright. CRITICAL re-export (R2): parser.rs exposes `pub(crate) fn duration_text` (line 1583) and printer.rs imports it via `use crate::parser::duration_text;` (printer.rs line 8); printer.rs is OUT OF SCOPE and MUST NOT be edited, so parser/mod.rs MUST re-export duration_text (`pub(crate) use`) to keep `crate::parser::duration_text` resolving byte-identically.
brief.context:
Third of five single-file sub-briefs decomposed from AWL0-REFAC-001 (see AWL-EXECUTION-PLAN.md Wave 0 and BRIEF-CRAFT.md entry 10), stamped from the 001a/001b templates after both landed clean — 001a (checker split) run a96973cb merged adb6e4cf, 001b (lib.rs split + 4-attr purge) run 3d201c36 merged 80426594. crates/aion-awl/src/parser.rs is 1585 lines, over the 500-line limit, and carries at line 1 a multi-line lint-bypass block `#![allow(missing_docs, clippy::expect_used, clippy::too_many_lines, clippy::unnecessary_wraps, clippy::unwrap_used)]` that this brief PURGES by doing the deferred work. Unlike 001b's FOUR vestigial attributes (deletable with clippy staying green, zero restructuring), 001c's block hides 32 REAL clippy errors — authoring measured them verbatim (see notes TECHNIQUE). This brief splits parser.rs into a thin parser/ folder module of named children AND does the purge work: 3 real doc comments (missing_docs), one needless-Result removal on a PRIVATE fn (unnecessary_wraps), one 223-line function decomposition (too_many_lines on parse_step), and 27 unwrap/expect eliminations via real ParseError propagation or restructuring. parser.rs is DELETED and replaced by the folder (lib.rs's `mod parser;` resolves it), so 001c mirrors 001a's shape, including 001a's path-exclude scope fence.

INTENTION: Third stamp of the decomposed pipe-cleaner sequence (001a..e). The monolithic AWL0-REFAC-001 failed twice — once by fraud (include! splices + crate-wide allow), once by an honest rollback after cascading errors mid-split of four files at once. This sub-brief is sized to the fix-cycle envelope (one 1585-line file); every non-negotiable is a mechanical gate (three GREEN ON BASE, one — split-complete — RED ON BASE because it is the work); the scope fence makes wandering impossible; and the full measured clippy inventory is embedded below so the developer never has to discover the fallout.

CONSTRAINTS:
- CN1: Canonical docs (AWL-EXECUTION-PLAN.md, AWL-0-SPEC-DRAFT.md, AWL-BC-DESIGN-DRAFT.md, AWL-UX.md, examples/) are the reading list; the plan's Wave 0 spec for this sub-brief is the CONTRACT — implement it verbatim. Discovered ambiguities are findings to report, never license to improvise.
- CN2: Coding standards (CLAUDE.md): no #[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore] bypasses, thiserror in libraries, no unwrap()/expect()/panic!() in library code, files <=500 lines, mod.rs = declarations/re-exports only, no silent failures.
- CN3: Gate argv, run EXACTLY in order before reporting: mechanical gates (no-bypasses-scoped, lints-pinned, scope-fence, split-complete) -> cargo fmt -> cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings -> cargo test -p aion-awl -> cargo test -p aion-cli.
- CN4: Round-trip property is sacred: parse . print = id and print . parse . print = print, byte-level, for every fixture. This refactor must not perturb it (the scope fence makes perturbing it impossible without failing a gate).
- CN5: Review lenses: the standard adversarial set PLUS a spec-fidelity lens — one reviewer reads the named plan section and the committed brief and checks the implementation clause by clause against R1-R4, including the docs-quality clause (placeholder docs are a blocking finding).
- CN6: Bar reminders: stage explicit paths (never `git add -A`); inspect with `git diff --no-ext-diff`; NEVER trust the workflow's own green — the operator re-runs the gates by hand before merge.

The full R1-R4 contract with acceptance criteria is committed at docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/briefs/AWL0-REFAC-001c-input.json — read it in the workspace.
pointers: docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/briefs/AWL0-REFAC-001a-input.json (the pilot's contract — parser.rs is deleted+foldered exactly like checker.rs was)
docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/BRIEF-CRAFT.md (why the gates are shaped this way; entries 8-12 and the run a96973cb / 3d201c36 validation notes)
crates/aion-awl/src/parser.rs (the 1585-line file to split + purge; DELETE it, replaced by parser/)
crates/aion-awl/src/checker/ and crates/aion-awl/src/lexer/ (the two landed folder-module layouts to mirror — private folder module, mod.rs = declarations/re-exports only)
crates/aion-awl/src/lib.rs (crate root — DO NOT EDIT; `mod parser;` line 10 already resolves the folder; `pub use parser::{ParseError, parse};` line 22 unchanged)
crates/aion-awl/src/printer.rs (DO NOT EDIT; line 8 `use crate::parser::duration_text;` must keep resolving via a parser/mod.rs re-export)
scope_in: crates/aion-awl/src/parser.rs (delete, replaced by the folder module)
crates/aion-awl/src/parser/ (create: mod.rs + responsibility-named children, each <=500 lines; mod.rs = declarations/re-exports only, including the pub(crate) duration_text re-export)
scope_out: REAL NAMED MODULES ONLY: every child file is a genuine `mod` whose name describes its responsibility. include!() textual splicing is FORBIDDEN. Mechanically-named files (part_00.rs, chunk_*.rs, body.rs-holding-everything) are REJECTED regardless of line counts. Dispatch 1 of the parent brief was rejected for include!("parts/part_00.rs") splices behind 2-line mod.rs files.

PURGE, DON'T RELOCATE: the line-1 allow block is deleted and the work behind each lint is DONE. No new #![allow]/#[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore] anywhere in parser/, and crates/aion-awl/Cargo.toml stays byte-identical — dispatch 2 of the parent brief was rejected for replacing `[lints] workspace = true` with a private allow-list. Both escape hatches are gated mechanically.

lib.rs MUST NOT be edited: `mod parser;` (line 10) already resolves the folder module; `pub use parser::{ParseError, parse};` (line 22) is unchanged. If you believe an edit outside the fence is required, STOP and report it as a finding instead of making it.

printer.rs MUST NOT be edited: it imports `use crate::parser::duration_text;` (line 8). Keep that path resolving by re-exporting duration_text (`pub(crate) use`) from parser/mod.rs — do NOT touch printer.rs. The scope-fence gate fails on any printer.rs edit.

ZERO behavior change: the split is a pure move; the only logic edits permitted are those strictly required to eliminate the five bypassed lints (doc comments, needless-Result removal on a private fn, parse_step helper extraction, unwrap/expect propagation). No diagnostic-text change, no span change, no AST-shape change, no verdict change. Round-trip property (CN4) untouched.
Existing tests, fixtures, goldens, and every non-parser file UNMODIFIED — enforced by the scope-fence gate.

Pre-existing bypass attributes ELSEWHERE in the crate (ast.rs missing_docs, printer.rs missing_docs, three test files) are OUT OF SCOPE — they belong to sub-briefs 001d/001e. Leave them exactly as they are; the gates here are scoped so they do not fire on them, and the scope fence forbids touching them.
SHALL NOT touch any crate other than aion-awl, nor anything in aion-awl outside src/parser*.
acceptance: no-bypasses-scoped gate (GREEN on base): grep over the landed-clean paths (checker, lib.rs, lexer) plus parser/ once present returns no allow/expect/ignore/include! matches. This gate scans only EXISTING paths (it does not name the deleted checker.rs or parser.rs), so it is reliable and green on base and goes red on any real bypass.
lints-pinned gate (GREEN on base): crates/aion-awl/Cargo.toml has exactly one [lints] section, containing workspace = true.
scope-fence gate: git diff versus the pinned base SHA, excluding crates/aion-awl/src/parser.rs and crates/aion-awl/src/parser, is empty.
split-complete gate (RED on base by design): parser.rs gone AND parser/mod.rs present AND every parser/*.rs at or under 500 lines AND zero bypass attributes under parser/.

cargo fmt -> cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings -> cargo test -p aion-awl -> cargo test -p aion-cli, all clean. (The full workspace suite is run by the operator at merge review while tasks #243/#244 are open.)

Hand review: parser/mod.rs is declarations + re-exports only (including the pub(crate) duration_text re-export); child names are honest; the line-1 block is gone with the work genuinely done (docs are real informative sentences, zero unwrap/expect remain, parse_step is decomposed under 100 lines, parse_maybe_about returns Option); `pub use parser::{ParseError, parse};` and `crate::parser::duration_text` both byte-compatible; printer.rs/lib.rs/ast.rs/checker/lexer untouched.
brief.notes:
DISPATCH HISTORY (read this first): the parent brief AWL0-REFAC-001 was dispatched twice and REJECTED twice at operator review, then decomposed into five single-file sub-briefs. Dispatch 1 faked the split with include!("parts/part_00.rs") textual splices behind 2-line mod.rs files plus a crate-wide #![allow(...)]. Dispatch 2 rolled the split back after cascading errors and instead replaced aion-awl's Cargo.toml `[lints] workspace = true` with a private allow-list to silence clippy. BOTH escape hatches are now mechanical gates that run before anything expensive; do the real work. The pilots 001a (checker split) and 001b (lib.rs split + 4-attr purge) then landed FIRST-PASS clean (runs a96973cb / 3d201c36; 8/8 gates, 4/4 lenses, zero deviations each) — this brief is stamped from them. Honest reporting was noted favourably both times: if something genuinely cannot be done within the boundaries, say so plainly in your report rather than working around a gate.

TECHNIQUE: parser.rs is DELETED and replaced by a private parser/ folder module — lib.rs already declares `mod parser;` (line 10) which resolves parser/mod.rs automatically, so you need NO lib.rs edit and the scope-fence gate fails if you make one. Mirror the two landed layouts (checker/, lexer/): a folder whose mod.rs is `mod <child>;` declarations + `pub use`/`pub(crate) use` re-exports ONLY, with children named for responsibility, each <=500 lines. Split the large sub-parsers across child modules with multiple impl blocks (multiple `impl LineParser` / `impl ExprParser` / `impl TypeParser` across files of one crate is legal Rust and the intended approach — 001a did exactly this for the 530-line Ctx impl). Free helper functions shared across children need `pub(super)` (or `pub(crate)`) visibility — an internal widening, not a public-API/signature change. Natural seams: ParseError + impls (error.rs); SourceLine/SourceLines + count_indent/split_code_comment/trim_comment/span/lex_at/rebase_span (source.rs); DeclPhase + LineParser + cursor methods + parse_document/parse_workflow/parse_maybe_about/parse_io/parse_type_decl/parse_action_decl (document.rs); parse_step + parse_handler + field helpers (steps.rs); parse_do/parse_call_text/comma_parts (calls.rs); TypeParser + parse_type_at (types.rs); ExprParser + parse_expr_at + unit_text/duration_text (expressions.rs) — or an equivalent honest arrangement; each file <=500 lines. RE-EXPORT TRAP: `pub(crate) fn duration_text` (line 1583) is consumed by printer.rs (line 8, `use crate::parser::duration_text;`), which is OUT OF SCOPE — parser/mod.rs MUST `pub(crate) use` duration_text so `crate::parser::duration_text` still resolves; forget this and printer.rs (which you may not edit) will not compile.

MEASURED CLIPPY INVENTORY (authoring, on base 86cdd26f2e4b9a9731011af7f93dea3b56eff6d3, running `cargo clippy -p aion-awl --all-targets -- -D warnings` with ONLY the line-1 block deleted): 32 errors across five lints — you resolve every one WITHOUT introducing any bypass. parser.rs has NO #[cfg(test)] tests, so ALL sites are production paths.
  * missing_docs — 3 items: `pub struct ParseError` (line 21) and its fields `pub span: Span` (line 22) and `pub message: String` (line 23). Write REAL, informative doc sentences (what a ParseError is; what `span` locates and what `message` conveys). Placeholder docs (`The span.`, restating the field name) are a BLOCKING review finding.
  * clippy::unnecessary_wraps — 1 item: `fn parse_maybe_about(&mut self, indent: usize) -> Result<Option<AboutDecl>, ParseError>` (line 456/464). PRIVATE method, sole caller parse_document (`let about = self.parse_maybe_about(0)?;`), never returns Err — change the return to `Option<AboutDecl>` and drop the `?`. Private, so this is an in-scope signature change; NO public fn triggers this lint (authoring-verified — so the STOP-on-public-rename condition does NOT fire).
  * clippy::too_many_lines — 1 item: `fn parse_step` (line 614/622), measured 223/100 lines. The file-module split does NOT fix this (parse_step is a single function) — decompose it into cohesive behavior-preserving helpers (e.g. a step-field accumulator with a parse-one-field method) until <=100 lines. parse_step is the ONLY too_many_lines site (parse_document, parse_action_decl, etc. are already under the threshold — authoring-verified).
  * clippy::expect_used (10 sites) + clippy::unwrap_used (17 sites) — 27 production sites, ALL invariant-guaranteed, in two patterns. Eliminate by real ParseError propagation or restructuring — NEVER leave unwrap/expect, NEVER use `.unwrap_or*` swallow hacks, NEVER invent a new error type (ParseError is the existing one). Pattern A, peek-then-bump (`self.bump().expect("peeked")`, `self.peek().unwrap()`) at lines 389, 461, 480, 502, 534, 577, 615, 639, 802, 855, and the op-span 1321: each parse_X re-consumes a line the caller already peeked — the file ALREADY has the sanctioned helper `bump_required(&mut self, msg) -> Result<SourceLine, ParseError>` (line 935, used by parse_workflow); extend it (`self.bump_required(...)?`) or fold peek+bump into one fallible step. Pattern B, strip_prefix-after-match (`line.code.strip_prefix("when").unwrap()` and siblings) at lines 463-466, 482, 504, 536, 645-648, 660, 684, 717, 773, 880, 952, 964, 973, 991, 1135: each is guarded by a preceding `first_word(...)=="<kw>"` classification so the prefix is present — capture the remainder without unwrap (bind it alongside the keyword classification, or propagate a ParseError on None). Prefer a restructuring that removes the Option over fabricating an unreachable-but-misleading error message.
SIZING: 32 errors, but NOT 32 independent fixes — 5 are trivial (3 docs + 1 private unwrap + parse_step is one decomposition) and the 27 unwrap/expect collapse into TWO repeating patterns (establish the fix once, the rest are mechanical). Authoring judges this fits one dispatch (1 dev round + 3 fix cycles), on par with 001b plus genuine purge work; it is the heaviest of the three, so if a fix cycle is consumed by the parse_step decomposition, report an honest partial rather than a rushed one — the operator would rather redispatch than merge a behavior regression.

GATES: all mechanical gates except split-complete were verified GREEN on the base ref at authoring, so any red in those is yours; split-complete is RED on base BY DESIGN (parser.rs is 1585 lines, exists, and carries the block) and turns green only when parser.rs is gone, parser/mod.rs exists, every parser/*.rs is <=500 lines, and zero bypasses remain under parser/ — that is the work. NOTE on gate 1 (no-bypasses-scoped): it now ALSO guards the two LANDED splits (checker/, lexer/) and the crate root (lib.rs) so they stay clean, plus parser/ once you create it. It deliberately scans only paths that EXIST — it does NOT name the deleted checker.rs or the deleted parser.rs — because a nonexistent path makes grep exit with an error code that the leading `!` would flip into a false green (authoring found the 001a/001b `checker.rs`-with-`-s` form is unreliable on this operator's grep, which is ugrep 7.5.0; this brief's gate 1 is the corrected, reliable form and was verified to go RED on a synthetic bypass in parser/ and in lexer/). The scope-fence gate is pinned to a base SHA: the literal string PINNED_SHA_AT_DISPATCH in the scope-fence argv MUST be replaced by the operator with `git rev-parse main` at dispatch time (re-pin to fresh main HEAD), and the operator re-verifies gates 1-3 green + gate 4 red on that base before dispatching. The test gates are scoped (aion-awl + aion-cli) and deterministic — the flaky workspace suite is deliberately NOT a gate here (tasks #243/#244: aion-package `append_run_regenerates_type_checking_gleam` and aion-server --lib both flake under load in crates this diff never touches); if a workspace test outside aion-awl/aion-cli ever runs and flakes while every in-scope gate passes and your diff touches only crates/aion-awl/src/parser*, re-run once and report the flake honestly rather than attempting an out-of-scope fix. The operator runs the full workspace suite by hand at merge review. AUTHORING VERIFICATION (clean detached worktree on base 86cdd26f2e4b9a9731011af7f93dea3b56eff6d3, PINNED_SHA substituted to that SHA): no-bypasses-scoped exit 0, lints-pinned exit 0, scope-fence exit 0 on the clean tree (and correctly exit 1 on a synthetic out-of-scope printer.rs edit); split-complete exit 1 on base (parser.rs present); with a synthetic in-scope split simulated, scope-fence stayed 0 and split-complete flipped to 0, and gate 1 went RED on a synthetic bypass planted in parser/ and separately in lexer/.

gates.name: no-bypasses-scoped
argv: ps="crates/aion-awl/src/checker crates/aion-awl/src/lib.rs crates/aion-awl/src/lexer"; [ -e crates/aion-awl/src/parser ] && ps="$ps crates/aion-awl/src/parser"; ! grep -rnE '#!?\[(allow|expect)|#\[ignore|include!\(' $ps

gates.name: lints-pinned
argv: test "$(grep -c '^\[lints' crates/aion-awl/Cargo.toml)" -eq 1 && grep -A1 '^\[lints\]$' crates/aion-awl/Cargo.toml | grep -q '^workspace = true'

gates.name: split-complete
argv: test ! -f crates/aion-awl/src/parser.rs && test -f crates/aion-awl/src/parser/mod.rs && wc -l crates/aion-awl/src/parser/*.rs | awk '$2!="total" && $1>500{bad=1; print "OVER 500:", $0} END{exit bad}' && ! grep -rnE '#!?\[(allow|expect)|#\[ignore' crates/aion-awl/src/parser

lenses.name: correctness
lenses.charter: Hunt defects in the change itself: logic errors, unhandled cases, broken invariants, concurrency hazards, silent failure paths. Assume the change is wrong and try to construct the concrete input or state that breaks it. Pay special attention to the unwrap/expect eliminations (a propagated error path that is now reachable when it was not, or a restructuring that changes which branch runs) and to the parse_step decomposition (an extracted helper that mutates accumulator state in a different order).

lenses.name: regressions
lenses.charter: Hunt collateral damage: behaviour the diff changes that the brief did not ask to change. Read the surrounding code and its callers; name the existing behaviour at risk and the concrete scenario that regresses. The round-trip property (parse . print = id) and every diagnostic message/span are the invariants most at risk from the bypass-purge edits.

lenses.name: brief_compliance
lenses.charter: Hold the diff to the brief, adversarially: every acceptance criterion either demonstrably met or a blocking finding; every scope_out boundary respected; every claimed deviation actually declared. Pass-and-still-wrong is the failure class here — do not accept a claim without evidence in the diff or gate output.

lenses.name: spec_fidelity
lenses.charter: Read AWL-EXECUTION-PLAN.md Wave 0 (the AWL0-REFAC-001c entry — 'purges the parser.rs:1 allow block; docs written, unwrap/expect eliminated, long fns split') and the committed brief docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/briefs/AWL0-REFAC-001c-input.json, and check the implementation clause by clause against R1-R4: (R1) parser.rs replaced by a parser/ folder module of REAL responsibility-named children each <=500 lines, mod.rs = declarations/re-exports only, the large sub-parsers split across modules by concern using multiple impl blocks, lib.rs untouched; (R2) zero behavior/API change — `pub use parser::{ParseError, parse};` unchanged AND `crate::parser::duration_text` still resolves (parser/mod.rs re-exports the pub(crate) fn) so printer.rs compiles UNMODIFIED, tests/fixtures/goldens byte-identical, round-trip intact; (R3) the line-1 allow block PURGED by doing the work — the 3 missing_docs items carry REAL informative doc sentences (placeholder/uninformative docs like 'The span.' are a BLOCKING finding), parse_maybe_about's needless Result removed (private fn), parse_step decomposed to <=100 lines by behavior-preserving helper extraction, all 27 unwrap/expect eliminated via real ParseError propagation or restructuring (no `.unwrap_or*` swallow hacks, no invented error type, no relocated bypass), Cargo.toml [lints] byte-identical (workspace = true only), clippy -D warnings green; (R4) the diff touches ONLY parser paths. include!() splices, part_NN/chunk/body.rs naming, ANY lint-policy weakening (source attrs OR manifest OR a bypass relocated rather than purged), any unwrap/expect left behind or swallowed, any edit to printer.rs/lib.rs, or any change outside the fence are automatic blocking findings — both prior dispatches of the parent brief were rejected for the fraud patterns. Verify child-module names honestly describe their contents and that the parser module stays private + re-exported.
